evidences.title: Monad Token Presale Surpasses $128M Ahead of Mainnet Launch
evidences.content: The sale is the first direct token offering hosted on Coinbase. It is open to participants in over 80 countries, including the U.S. Early-stage activity was steady but slower than expected. Analysts attribute this to investors waiting for final moments to commit larger funds. Currently, the sale sees an additional $693,500 added each hour. The public sale will close on 23 November at 10:00, just ahead of Monad’s mainnet launch on 24 November 2025. ## What Monad Offers Monad is a high-performance Layer 1 blockchain. It is fully compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM). It is designed to reach 10,000 transactions per second with near-instant finality. Locked Tokens Tokens for all investors and team members, along with the Category Labs Treasury, are locked on Day 1 of Monad Public Mainnet and are subject to defined unlock and vesting schedules to ensure long-term alignment with the success of the project. All tokens in these categories are locked for a minimum of one year following initial token distribution as part of the launch of Monad Public Mainnet in November 2025, with further unlock schedules varying among group 6 members, as described herein. At the start of Monad Public Mainnet, 50.6B [...] herein. At the start of Monad Public Mainnet, 50.6B tokens (50.6%) will be locked. Locked tokens cannot be staked. The intent of this is twofold: to ensure that staking rewards initially contribute to the public circulation of MON and to avoid an initial concentration of rewards among those individuals (team members and investors) involved directly with the project. evidences.title: Monad Raises 269 Million From Token Sale, Mainnet Launch Set
evidences.content: #### #### Funding and Token Sale: What’s Going On Monad has completed its token sale on Coinbase, raising $269 million from over 85,000 participants. The Ethereum-compatible layer-1 blockchain exceeded its initial fundraising goal of $187 million. The public token sale itself offers 7.5 billion MON (7.5 % of total supply) at $0.025 per token, implying a fully diluted valuation of roughly $2.5 billion if fully subscribed. The sale is being hosted on Coinbase’s newly launched regulated token-launch platform, accessible in more than 80 countries including the U.S. Participation rules include a minimum bid of $100, a maximum bid of $100,000, and a special allocation algorithm that prioritizes smaller bids to encourage broad retail access. [...] logo # Monad Raises 269 Million From Token Sale, Mainnet Launch Set For Nov 24 ## Monad Raises Massive Funding From Token Public Sale, Mainnet Launches Today The Layer-1 ecosystem is heating up again, and one of the most closely-watched networks of 2025 has just taken a major leap forward. MON token from Monad recently raised hundreds of millions in its public token sale ahead of the mainnet launch on November 24, 2025. The fundraising, sale...

evidences.title: [PDF] monad / mon token – coinbase token sales disclosure1
evidences.content: Sale: up to 7.5B MON (7.5% of initial total supply). Sale Price: $0.025 per MON. Implied Fully Diluted Value (FDV) of the Monad Network: $2.5 billion. 2 Determination of the Sale Price: Prior to the public sale on Coinbase token sales, there has been no public market for MON tokens. The price of $0.025 per MON token in the public sale is a fixed price based on a $2.5 billion fully diluted network valuation and an initial total supply of 100 billion MON tokens. The sale price was determined through a structured approach that considers several key factors, including market conditions, competitive positioning, and strategic objectives. This methodology ensures that the sale price reflects both the intrinsic value of the Monad network and broader market dynamics. evidences.title: Monad Launches Public Sale on Coinbase's New Token Sale Platform
evidences.content: We view the demand profile for Monad fitting into this assertion. In absolute terms, raising around $140 million from a retail-facing public sale before mainnet launch is a strong result, especially for a first offering on a new token launch platform. The initial sale started fast, with roughly half the MON public allocation subscribed within hours, then slowed as more price-sensitive and more cautious buyers took time to evaluate the deal. In relative terms, this is very different from MegaETH, which filled its fundraising cap within minutes. MegaETH, however, used a floating price and FDV auction design with lockups that rewarded long-term participants, including a mandatory one-year lockup for US investors and optional lockups for others. Monad instead opted for a fixed price, fixed [...] for others. Monad instead opted for a fixed price, fixed FDV structure at $2.5 billion with no lockups for public sale buyers. The contrast reinforces our view that the marginal buyer today is more discriminating on structure and risk, and that projects cannot assume automatic oversubscription simply because they are L1s with strong backers. [...] Monad’s offering follows the familiar high F...

evidences.title: Monad Just Lost 52% Since Launch: Why Smart Money Is Watching ...
evidences.content: The same ICO that was supposed to be the smart play for generating memecoin capital just became a cautionary tale about why tokenomics matter more than technology. And the irony? While Monad bleeds, random dog coins are still pumping 10x with zero utility. Here’s what actually happened, why it matters for anyone still holding MON, and what this tells us about the next wave of ICO plays versus just sticking with memecoins. ## The Hype Was Real Monad launched with everything going for it. $225 million raised from Paradigm and top VCs. Promises of 10,000 TPS with full Ethereum compatibility. Coinbase Launchpad’s first major ICO in six years. An oversubscribed sale that pulled in $269 million from 85,800 participants. [...] Press enter or click to view image in full size Press enter or click to view image in full size ## What Went Wrong: The Tokenomics Trap The problem wasn’t the technology. Monad’s tech is legit. The issue was the tokenomics structure that everyone ignored while chasing the hype. Only 10% to 11% of the total 100 billion token supply was circulating at launch. That created a $2.5 billion fully diluted valuation sitting on top of extremely thin liquidity. This is the...

evidences.title: Phishing and social engineering drain $600M from web3 in H1 2025 ...
evidences.content: Smart contract exploits are not the only threat. In 2025, people have become one of the weakest links. Why? In the first half of this year alone, phishing and social engineering drained ~$600M across web3. And it’s already more than all of 2024’s full-year totals. Here are more numbers: • $476M were lost in total in Q2 2025. It's up 4x from Q1 of the same year. • $330M Bitcoin were lost after sharing the wallet access. • $100M+ of high-net-worth Coinbase users were also lost after attackers posed as "Coinbase support", quoting real balances to build trust. And that’s just the tip of the iceberg: fake wallet-permission requests, malicious token approvals, and wallet-draining scripts in cloned dApps are everywhere. Why does it matter? Social engineering doesn’t attack code, 𝗶𝘁 𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗮𝗰𝗸𝘀 [...] 10 1 Comment Like Comment To view or add a comment, sign in Ee Sheng Goh + Report this post [...] 1 Like Comment To view or add a comment, sign in Ville ᴋᴏᴄʜ + Report this post
